description: Select the smallest validation command set that covers changed code paths before broadening to package-level gates.
body: Use changed files, nearby tests, package scripts, and ownership boundaries to choose focused validation first. Output the command, expected coverage, and when to broaden. This skill exposes capability only; it must not run commands without runtime permission. Use when core engineering behavior work touches repository evidence, git state, user instructions, and focused validation output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include git status, focused tests, typecheck output, command logs, and unchanged-user-work checks. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves overwriting user work, broad rewrites, stale assumptions, or unvalidated completion claims, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
